submission.title: How is everyone liking S4 so far? (Without Spoiling)
submission.body: Haven’t watched S4 yet, gonna wait until November and watch the whole thing at once. I’m just curious how everybody is liking it, is it bad now that the original plot ended with S3 or is the show still in its prime?

comments.body: I rewatched the series after pt.1. I love it and there aren’t other interesting shows (imo) with young characters and adventures like this which I look forward to. People upset of the lack of treasure hunting I understand because that’s the basis of the show but I’m also not mad about it, I really love just watching the pouges lives! ![gif](emote|free_emotes_pack|slightly_smiling)

submission.title: Feel sad for Chandler!
submission.body: That was a beautiful dance, and I definitely felt emotion from her! I love Stephen but there were quite a few wobbles and it wasn’t my favourite of his so far. To give him a 9 and that feedback whilst Chandler got all 8s and told to dig deeper felt very unfair to me. I really hope she gets the votes she needs to stay in the competition as I really like her 💔

comments.body: Going first didn’t help 😔
I agree that he didn’t deserve that 9 but I don’t think Chandler did either, Danny’s contemporary really showed that depth that she was lacking imo
I agree, so many wobbles and timing issues in his compared to Chandler’s. No reason to boo CA for her critiques, that was super weird!
I felt like Chandler always puts her best. I felt like the judges were saying “dig deeper” as in it wasn’t good enough. I felt like she deserved a higher score
Agreed, I feel like these scores are a smidge inflated. I didnt think Phaedra should have received all 8’s. Danny’s so far is the best of the night for me
Who are you voting for right now?

What pisses me off the most was the fact that they were calling out Chandler for mistakes on Brandon's part, which baffles me, especially when Val is not giving Phaedra full routines or has her stand for 10 bars.
I think the scoring is all over the place already tonight, I agree Danny was amazing!
Chandler and Danny so far! I think her having similar scores to Dwight and Phaedra and below Danny puts her at risk as she doesn’t have as large of a fan base.
I dont understand why they gave Phaedra 8 😂 she cannot dance 😭
[deleted]
Her last week criticism was all been about hitting sharp 🙃🙃🙃

It’s frustrating especially since this routine had a lot of contemporary content and showcased her beautiful ballet technique. I wish that was acknowledged more because I feel like a lot of non dancers who watch the show think contemporary is “easy” or a filler dance. Unfortunately I think the lifts killed the momentum a bit but not enough to not earn at least one 9.
I am excusing this as an apology to Phaedra for Gene's scores last time.
Sadly, I think the judges are trying to get rid of Chandler. I am not sure why, but other than that, she is not a producer favorite. I can not stand the judging. I feel terrible for Chandler.
It depends on the dance style.

Chandler is a technically beautiful dancer but contemporary unfortunately doesn't have a lot of technical stuff to judge. It's basically just on feel. Stephens dance had much harder choreo this week then his previous weeks which he executed so I don't think it's all that shocking he received a higher score than he did last week. I think Chandler could've gotten a 10 with a more technical dance

Tbh if that last lift had gone more smoothly & she hadn’t opened the show (which is when judges tend to be pickier) I think she’d have gotten at least one or two nines.

She did really well and should still be proud of herself, and I’m hoping she’s still got enough support to get through the week despite not being as high up on the leaderboard tonight.
Yeah considering what Chandler struggles with is the emotional connection element it makes sense contemporary would fall flat.

I love Stephen and think he’s been underscored in recent weeks but I honestly noticed a few wobbles in that Argentine Tango! The choreo was tricky and you can tell he is trying super hard, I just don’t think he executed it in a way that matches his scores tonight

I don’t know anything about contemporary but I will say there was a noticeable difference between Witney and Chandler doing the same style.  And obv Witney is a professional but it wasn’t Chandlers best dance imo
100%!! Hope she’s not too hard on herself
Phaedra hardly did anything.
She did nothing in that dance.

I didn't really see depth from Danny honestly. He's very stoic when he dances, and in general. Chandler is much more expressive in her face and body and fluid in her movements. I also think Chandler's dance was more lighthearted and maybe that's why it didnt feel "deep"? That being said, I loved Danny's dance, so I think he deserved the scores he got. I think Chandler deserved 9s despite the stumble. Phaedra also got 8s, and chandler had much more movement and overall better quality dancing than her IMO.
Seriously. Like Chandler is the one that got a real score tonight - everyone else has been overscored as fuck. It’s suspicious.
They’re being wayyyy too harsh on chandler
No it wasn’t and the scores she got were accurate… except everyone else has been overscored and putting her unfairly at the bottom of the pack. Very nervous about this.
The “dig deeper” frustrated me, I think she is truly doing the best she can mentally and physically. She did the same thing with charity every week
Danny’s performance was hands down the best of the night.. but No way was Chandlers and Brookes the same Brooke should have gotten 7s
I’m not nervous about Chandler until brooks and phaedra are both gone
I’m voting for Chandler, Danny, Ilona and Phaedra

I’m so PISSED!!  I live in Dallas and just realized they started DWTS at the halfway point because of the Cruz/Allred debate on ABC!!  It started exactly at Derek and Haley’s dance!  I thought they were just starting later!! So pissed at WFAA in Dallas!!!  😡😡😡

Chandler started off miles ahead of everyone else, and the others have caught up.  This was not her strongest dance.  She got out of sync and didn't fully finish her moves.  This was Stephen's best dance and he deserved high scores.
I can’t believe Chandler in was the bottom 3 tonight
brooks and phaedra were both insanely over scored
I hope they're just trying to create a comeback arc for her to push more votes

What do you mean not  a lot of technical stuff? Contemporary you have to have ballet feet and legs and arms in order to make the movement special.There are a number of elements in contemporary that frankly everyone on the panel knows because they all dance contemporary. I felt a little verklempt when they danced I thought she dug in. The mess up on the lift would take it to a 9 but wow did they ever piss all over that. Chandler was emotional speaking about her mom and I thought she conveyed that bond.
This is the same bs and yet Chandler is such an amazing dancer that the show wants to kill that confidence she has in her work.

Idk if you all are following Lindsay on socials but I remember seeing a video she posted a few weeks ago on tiktok. It was facts about the behind the scenes of dwts and she kind explained how the placement and lineup of the dances are chosen and how it can effect the judges scores. If you eat that and then reflect on the scores given, it does give a little perspective. Nonetheless, I’m sad that chandler was given low scores as her performance was amazing as usual and I agree, Stephan needed more practice this week. I also feel bad that she had to go through being in the bottom 3 this week when I thought she did really well
That's on Val. Tbh, his choreography has really dipped low this year, and I think she knows he's undervalued her ability.
Regardless, even if it is on him, I can only judge on what I watched. She did very little and was overscored.
I admit that assessment is correct, but it's kinda hard not to excuse it after she was pretty much singled out by Gene.
The people love to boo CA lol

As a Stephen fan I agree. I felt like the judges were too harsh on him in previous dances and were starting to piss me off but they were almost too nice on this one lol. He definitely had some wobbles/stiffness and I was ready to hear the judges critique him. The choreo was really tough though, so maybe that gave him some points. Happy he finally got a nine but was definitely surprised!
Agree OP - reminder to everyone in the comment section to continue to vote each week for our girl Chandler!!!! 💛💛
Stephen and Rylee. Outstanding!
Absolutely Stephen's best dance.
They mocked Gene and his ridiculous score. That was the right thing to do. I don't think overscoring her was the way to go.
Agree to disagree, I suppose. Overscored definitely, the why, obvious, but definitely can see why it'd be challenged.
I also think Stephen was overscored.
Absolutely, that 9 should've been Chandler's.
They don’t do them in any particular order. They just have them like that for dramatic tension.
Yep.
She definitely deserved multiple nines

u have to realize the different backgrounds of the two tho. Chandler is trained in contemporary, and has stayed the same since night 1. they judges keep asking for her to open up, and she has struggle to do so. I see exactly what they mean, too. 
Stephen has NEVER danced before, and this choreo was HARD. This was an 8/9 deserving dance. 
They score people differently, just like how Charli was held to a higher standard than the rest of the competition her season. She CONSTANTLY had to prove herself, and earn those 10s. Wayne or Selma aren’t going to be on the same grading scale as her, simply because she is trained. She has musicality & rhythm. She has the ability to connect with the music.
[deleted]
I agree

Her dancing is beautiful but I think she is becoming stagnant, a lot of the other contestants are having these big improvements and she just isn’t. Unfortunately sometimes when you start off really well like she did it can be hard to continue to stand out once everyone else starts to catch up. I think she is going to need a big moment or she is going to go home sooner rather than later.

I agree. I felt bad for Chandler because I do feel that she’s giving it her all. However Carrie Ann was right. She just, in my opinion, is missing that connection to the audience charisma. She is a very flawless dancer but I’m missing the connection to her for some reason. Like Danny and Whitney’s contemporary- I felt so connected to them, I felt the emotion.
Agreed!

erm this isn’t even true, and even if it was.. she is quite literally one of the stars of a movie that is 95% dance. 
the girl is TRAINED. She knows it & so do the judges, so idk why ur trying to act as if she isn’t at a technical advantage. 
she is a STUNNING dancer, but this week just was not her best. 
This is by far Stephen’s best dance technically wise, and that was a HARD routine. Like crazy hard. He did have a few wobbles, but honestly with how hard the choreo was, I can look past it.

She’s getting bussed. She had a better dance than every couple who scored the same or higher than her, except for Joey. She was first this week, one of the last declared safe, and I am pretty sure she’ll get saddled with Moana 2 next week. Plus ever since Joey took over the top spot last week, Bachelor Nation is awake and coming after her.

I think it made sense in comparison to his past dances bc of the content but yeah I think 8s make sense for this time he has improved on the critiques though. I think it's hard rn because all the scores for every couple are kinda meshed together
Eh. Danny was a prop for Witney. Don’t get me wrong it was still great to watch, but from a a dance standpoint on the 3 contestants, Chandler and Stephen did a lot more than Danny.
Agreed, if Len was on the panel he would prob say something about the lack of content for him
I loved chandlers dance and I preferred it over Danny’s. He did good too though….
This!! My immediate thought was that she’d score higher if she wasn’t first.
Yea gotta agree sometimes we don't know what the judges are seeing

chandler actually was still competing and placing fairly high in pretty big competitions till she was at least 14 (someone has posted about it on here before) she’s most definitely trained in contemporary. she’s obviously still amazing i’m not saying she’s not, but for the training she has i think the score was pretty accurate. i thought she would probably get a 9 but i’m not surprised by the 8s!
Chandlers mistake in the dance with the lift was kind of Brandon’s fault lol
Yup it is pretty annoying Brandon is making these mistakes as a pro
All he had to do was avoid lifts. He's not strong enough to handle a lift as comfortably as say Tony or Mark.
Isn’t she only 19 (edit: 24**) Perhaps it’s harder to be one of the younger contestants and convey emotion in a mature way. You have less life experience to draw from.

I think judges scores on abilities. Chandler is an incredible dancer and has known experience so she’s got to be judged harder than those who were never dancers in the first place. I think Stephen did so well applying what he was told from last week which is why he got higher scores. With that said, I too felt it was completely backhanded to tell chandler she didn’t dig deep when it was obviously a big deal to her. That should’ve never been the “critiques” she was given.

They have absolutely NOT ‘caught up.’ The people on this show who never danced before are not equal to a woman with multiple years of classical dance experience, no matter what the show is trying to say.
But that’s not a fair metric. An 8 for Stephen can be different than an 8 for Chandler, but at the end of the day, that 8 means the same thing when they’re tallying who goes home.

I don’t disagree! I’ve actually been so curious on how “scoring” works and how couples get eliminated. Like I understand it’s compiled of judges scores and public votes but really how do they equate? And exactly how are obviously great performances the same scores as not as good performances?

I respectfully disagree. Danny hardly did any dancing in that routine. It was nicely choreographed by Witney in a way that he hardly had to do anything while Witney did the actual dancing.

It was a nice performance I will give it that, but Danny didn’t play a big part in it.
He can only do so much with what he’s given. You can’t do beautiful choreography with a crummy dancer. Xochitl had much more capability so it allowed Val to create more.
There is zero chance Stephen could have done what chandler did in her routine but I bet chandler could have done what Stephen did better
This is my main gripe. Why do they act like they don’t want her there she is the best dancer by miles and miles.
She’s 24
I think Chandler is 24
Judging everyone on the same scale doesn’t kick in til about week 8. Right now scores are often for personal improvement. It’s a reality show first and a dance competition second.
She’s an actress. Come on, that’s what she’s supposed to do with characters. She’s not taking us on a journey, she’s performing well executed steps. That’s the issue.
She’s not making personal improvements. They tell her to show the emotion, become the character…. But she’s not quite there.

That’s what they said about Charli. When someone is MUCH better than everyone else they have to find something to say and that’s what they go to.

The judges shape the perception they’re going for too much.

Judge everyone fairly and give criticism fairly is what I would like to see. If you’re going to get on her about digging deeper but then give Stephen a 9 just throw away the numbering system.
It’s making me think of charity last season 😞 constantly being told to dig deeper bc the person dancing wasn’t enough

I honestly thought chandler was going to get at least one 10 but the judges seemed to have agreed that it wasn’t her best. I still thought her dance was beautiful though. I’m no pro dancer so maybe I’m wrong but hey I’m not a dance competition judge.

Yeah, but you can still make a beginner routine look competent, but Val isn't giving her basics. He's giving her varied levels of steps in focal points rather than giving her a routine she can handle and grow from. She has to be comfortable with fundamentals first, which she is not. You can compose a simple but effective routine that looks complete without losing the quality.

idk she came out and did the contemporary with this big fake smile on her face and a stiffness throughout her whole body as if she was performing for a disney holiday special. there was no emotion in the dance and i think that's why they scored her low

Personally I think Stephen did deserve that 9 since it felt like he was putting his all in it. I like chandler but I think she isn’t giving it her all yet. Also I only know of Chandler because of DWTS which I’ve heard a lot of people say so I can see her fan base being smaller compared to the other stars. Their dance felt more technical then emotional. I think having her in the “bottom three” is going to help her have a breakthrough moment like Ilona did. But all in all I’m just glad Brooke and Gleb are gone
Danny's contemp was bunch of lifts and 10s of dance. Get real. Looking constipated to show angst ain't no depth.

I don't think it was fake, however it has a lot less impact than harder emotions.

Also, DWTS has a hard on for grief, sadness, anxiety, etc vs any type of happy emotion. Especially for contemporary dances!
Good point! One would think it would help her to be an actress, to fully get into the dances.

Contemporary has a lot of technical stuff to judge, get Abby Lee miller to judge a contemporary and you’ll see. It’s just the judges on this show knowledge on the technicality of contemporary or even jazz dancing quite frankly just is no offense, weak…. So they only judge based off of feeling and to them contemporary routines are suppose to be sad feels. (To be fair Carrie Ann and Bruno usually base even their ballroom judging on feels and not technique). But let’s not say contemporary doesn’t have a lot of technical stuff to judge, it for sure does just like all other dance styles. Chandler had the best contemporary routine of the contemporaries done that night as it was more authentic contemporary style dancing. Danny’s routine was much more of a cabaret showcase routine than contemporary, which is what pros on this show choreograph more as Cabaret routines parading as contemporary is much more flashy so it’s more entertaining especially attached to an emotional piece of music. Chandler & Brandon choosing an upbeat cutesy song and not an emotional piece is why they got those 8s, bad tactics if they wanted great scores knowing how the judges judge. 

But as a contemporary dancer you hit a small nerve there because there’s literal BOOKS on contemporary technique that you can find online from regular book stores to the Juilliard campus, I know this is a ballroom show but jeez the disrespect to the contemporary genre is hurtful sometimes. Contemporary is like an extension of ballet, many techniques are similar to that and good contemporary is extremely difficult because it takes a lot of body discipline 😭. Contemporary is very technical, that’s like saying ballet isn’t very technical.
My bad! Someone else said she was that age. You’re right!

Oh my. Ok maybe I didn't word that well. 😭 I'm sure there is a lot technically that goes into contemporary but for dwts specifically as both the judges and the pros have much more background and understanding in Latin and ballroom, they don't seem to judge on technicality here and pros also don't choreograph on technicality like you said. For that reason I think Chandlers skill would be better judged in a dance where the judges can see her technical skills. Chandlers strong suit seems to be execution of dances over that intensity and angst the judges seem to look for in contemporary on dwts. I see why people oppose it being part of the show before the semi finals or finals because someone who doesn't do a technically great contemporary can get a higher score than someone who executes a slightly less emotional ballroom or Latin dance

submission.title: US to Scotland
submission.body: I’m moving to Edinburgh from the US. I am so excited but blind because I feel like I dont know about all the nuances of moving countries. What are all the things I need to do to cross my T’s and dot my I’s when it comes to leaving the US to another country???

comments.body: Making sure you have the right Visa

It’s a trivial thing, but make sure to get loyalty apps for all the supermarkets, otherwise everything is 3000% more expensive with zero justification.

Also, welcome to Edinburgh! Hope you enjoy your time here!
I’m on a partnership visa! Spent the last few years making sure it’d be the right one. Now wondering what I need to do to move countries properly (and hopefully permanently)

Thank you! 

So interesting we’ll have the same here with loyalty apps and I love them! Will be sure to get those

There’s lots of ‘expat’ groups on the internet / facebook - they’re probably your best source. Your immediate issues are going to be;
Bank account 
Mobile contract
Driving license if applicable 
Registering with a GP
Registering with a dentist 

Things you’re going to find strange 
Currency / money - you’ll try and convert everything to dollars. 
Weights and measures. We’re largely metric (grams, kilos) , apart from distance which is still in miles and weight of vehicles which is in tonnes. Temperatures we use degrees centigrade not degrees Fahrenheit. It’s an odd mixture

Ok, honestly? Avoid discussing Football (soccer) politics or independence.

A guaranteed way to start an argument
Oooh love football though (hearts fan already)! Other topics I’ll happily avoid !
Fine for an American to ask about those things. Anyone who gets offended by someone trying to understand our politics or culture is an idiot and isn’t worth speaking to.

If your partner is already here or is just a citizen then that makes life 100000x easier. Moving properly isn't that difficult past getting a mobile, bank account, and place to stay (this is really fucking hard on its own though). 

You will probably have a national insurance number on the back of your visa, but if you weren't given one you will need to apply to have the right to work. It's rough for partners to find work, or so my lazy ex said, but it's impossible without that NI number. 

Prepare that homesickness may creep in but it goes away as you make more connections. I genuinely had a degree of homesickness my first year or two here before I built a stable friend group and found my sense of community. Moving abroad is actually emotionally pretty difficult. 

Otherwise, best of luck and enjoy the ride! It's an amazing place, Edinburgh and Scotland in general.
Thank you! Thats what has been on my mind as well (besides GPs) which is a great shout!

Love this! Thank you, I plan to look for work as soon as I’m there. I already have a great support system there so I’m hoping to not have a hard time emotionally but still nervvy! 

I didn’t know I’d need an NI number so that’s something I’ll defo be checking for.

>hearts fan already

shaky start but you'll be okay eventually
Not like I’ll be switching to hibs ever. Never better !
No idea, but welcome. :)
Remember to use your inside voice.
Oh yes! I’ll make sure to make a scene when I arrive 🙄
I don’t mean to be an arse here, but outwith a few select US immigrants in this sub most people are Edinburgh residents. So not exactly gonna be brimming with knowledge on this topic…

>tonnes


Tonnes are metric
That’s a good point. I just didn’t know where else to add! Maybe will try other subreddits to add to.
You also need a license for a television.
Should have specified - some are not metric (ton = 2000lbs) and some are (metric tonne = 1000kg)

Amex let you bring your credit file over with you. So if you've time make sure to set up and amex account in the US and start the process to move it over to Amex UK. It'll make things like getting a mobile phone and credit much easier in a few years time.

The main thing you will struggle with is it like of address history for credit applications. Something as simple as a new mobile phone requires a credit check, and providing 3 years UK-based address history, which you will not be able to do. When my (now) wife move to the UK I had to take a phone contract out in my name for her, as they wouldn't give her one. It was about 2 to 2.5 years being in the UK before she could pass the necessary checks. 

This will apply to any credit application as it's part of the regulatory checks credit providers must complete prior to lending.
No specific advice to offer but best of luck, hope it goes well, I’m sure it will do.
No, you only need license if you are watching live TV or using iplayer.
That is so helpful! I’ve been wondering how the credit situation works/banking/phones etc. but knowing how long the credit situation is will defo help me manage those expectations. Thank you!
Thank you!!
Thank you!! (:

The main difference between the US and UK credit systems is whereas your credit score (the actual number) is vital in the US, the actual number here is 99% meaningless, and it's your credit history that matters, with each credit provider having their own criteria on whether you're eligible or not. 

You can check your credit score in the UK and it'll be as high as it can go but you're not guaranteed the credit.
Pretty sure only some supermarkets do that, Tesco being the worst offender.
Excellent, welcome!
